title: [cross-impl] DIO reserved byte handling inconsistent
description: Python (python/src/lichen/rpl/messages.py:169) now enforces data[7]==0 per RFC 6550 §6.3 in DIO.from_bytes (raises RplError). Rust (rust/lichen-rpl/src/message.rs:116-119 Dio::from_bytes skips index 7, takes dodag_id from 8..24) and Zephyr C (lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/messages.c:48-49 lichen_rpl_dio_parse reads flags[6], dodag[8..] ignores byte 7). All set byte 7=0 on tx (consistent). Different rejection behavior on non-zero reserved. Reviewed commits 2ff4962dd (Python RPL), cbf216397 (Zephyr COAP merge), and bd metadata. Other commits were bead auto-updates with no code impact.
close_reason: Fixed cross-impl DIO reserved byte validation per RFC 6550 §6.3 in Rust and C to match Python. Added checks returning InvalidOption / LICHEN_RPL_ERR_BAD_OPT on nonzero byte 7. Tests/vectors unchanged as they use zero. Commit 65ced09e1
